questions.question: What is the primary concept in Distributed Mutual Exclusion?
questions.explanation: Mutual Exclusion is the primary concept in Distributed Mutual Exclusion, where multiple processes cannot access shared resources simultaneously.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of distributed mutual exclusion?
questions.explanation: Write-Exclusive is a type of distributed mutual exclusion, where only one process can write to a resource at a time.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a token in distributed mutual exclusion?
options: A) To grant access to shared resources
B) To prevent access to shared resources
C) To synchronize access to shared resources
D) To coordinate access to shared resources
questions.explanation: A token is used to prevent access to shared resources by multiple processes, allowing only one process to access it at a time.

questions.question: Which algorithm is used to solve distributed mutual exclusion?
questions.explanation: Paxos is an algorithm used to solve distributed mutual exclusion, ensuring that only one process can access a shared resource at a time.

questions.question: What is the main characteristic of a distributed consensus algorithm?
options: D) Ensures all processes agree on a common value
questions.explanation: Distributed consensus algorithms ensure that all processes agree on a common value, making a collective decision on a shared resource.
